name: The Tipsy Gnome
description: A squat, round-roofed establishment two blocks south of the Bottoms Up on the lower stretch of Mill Road. The sign is a carved wooden gnome mid-stumble, one hand on a tankard and the other on a mushroom for balance. The building leans slightly to the left and has done so for decades, a structural tilt that the regulars consider characterful rather than concerning. Inside it smells of mushroom stew, pipe smoke, and the particular warmth of a room that has been heated by the same hearth since before anyone can remember. The ceiling is low — built for gnomes and dwarves, adapted for everyone else by the installation of beams that taller patrons learn to duck after the first visit.

sounds: The creak of the building settling — ongoing, ambient, ignored by regulars
Lell's lute from the corner booth, softer here than at the Bottoms Up
The thunk of heavy ceramic mugs on thick wooden tables
Mira's laugh from behind the bar, sudden and full
Bob humming in the kitchen, audible through the serving window
smells: Mushroom stew — always, regardless of the menu
Pipe smoke layered into the walls over decades
Fresh bread from the morning delivery
The particular warm-wood smell of a building that has been heated continuously for longer than anyone has been alive
atmosphere.lighting: Low, amber, candlelit. The windows are small and round, letting in more character than illumination. Two oil lamps behind the bar. The hearth provides most of the light and all of the warmth.

layout.name: Main Room
layout.description: Compact and circular, following the building's round footprint. Eight tables, none of them the same size. The bar curves along the north wall. The hearth dominates the center, a round stone structure that forces traffic to flow around it.

layout.name: The Bar
layout.description: A curved oak counter with four stools, each worn to the specific dimensions of its most frequent occupant. Mira Barrelbottom's new domain — smaller than the Bottoms Up bar but, she insists, better proportioned.

layout.name: Corner Booth
layout.description: A deep, padded booth against the south wall. Lell Sparrow's preferred performance spot. The acoustics here are surprisingly good, a fact Lell attributes to the building's tilt.

layout.name: Kitchen
layout.description: Gnome-proportioned, immaculate, and Bob the Porous's exclusive territory. The serving window is at bar height, which means Bob stands on a crate. The crate has a name. Bob will not share it.

layout.name: The Lean
layout.description: Three tables on the tilted side of the building, where drinks slide gently if unattended. Regulars use weighted coasters. Newcomers learn quickly.

layout.name: Upstairs
layout.description: Three small rooms for rent, accessible by a narrow spiral staircase. The rooms tilt with the building. Sleeping takes adjustment.

layout.name: Cellar
layout.description: Cooler than the Bottoms Up cellar, mushroom-damp, excellent for storing ale. Mira has already organized it to her specifications.

hooks: Mira brought her locked ledger from the Bottoms Up — her secrets moved with her
The building's tilt is getting worse. Very slowly. The regulars have started a betting pool on which direction it will go.
Lell Sparrow claims the acoustics in the corner booth are the best in Millhaven and is composing something he will not discuss
Bob the Porous has been experimenting with a new stew recipe that uses ingredients he will not name, sourced from a supplier he will not identify
An old map was found behind the hearthstones during renovation — Mira has it locked in the cellar

worldContext.location: The Tipsy Gnome, lower Mill Road, Millhaven
